title: phippsia capillaris subsp. pulvinata
intro:
Phippsia capillaris subsp. pulvinata, also known as Tibetan Club Moss, is a species of plant in the family Selaginellaceae, characterized by its small, scale-like leaves and ability to grow in high-altitude, cold environments.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phippsia capillaris subsp. pulvinata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the dried leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using a tincture or extract to create a concentrated medicinal product.
